mechanism_narrative: A downward move in FEDFUNDS signals easier discounting and lower funding stress, which lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is already restrictive.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and 20-day SPX returns are negative or below baseline in more than 60% of triggered observations, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE after a low-unemployment period signals weakening labor income and a higher recession probability, which depresses cyclical earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If labor-market deterioration episodes defined by a >0.3pp 3-month rise in UNRATE do not coincide with below-median 20-day SPX returns, or if returns are positive in over 55% of such episodes, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS after a weak sales base indicates improving nominal demand and revenue momentum, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through near-term earnings revisions.
failure_condition: If positive month-over-month RSAFS momentum fails to associate with positive 5-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate stays below 52% over any 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL is flat or falling, the economy is expanding without renewed inflation pressure, which lowers real-rate stress and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO was 102.6651 and CPIAUCSL was 307.917 on 2023-11-01
failure_condition: If the joint condition of rising INDPRO and non-accelerating CPIAUCSL does not outperform either proxy alone for 60-day SPX returns, or if median returns are not positive, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals housing demand exhaustion and tighter credit transmission, which weakens growth-sensitive equity sectors and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If HOUST declines greater than 5% month over month are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL deceleration combined with a still-elevated FEDFUNDS rate creates room for future policy easing, compressing discount rates and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL was 307.917 and FEDFUNDS was 5.33 on 2023-11-01
failure_condition: If CPI deceleration plus elevated Fed funds does not improve 60-day SPX returns relative to either condition alone, or if the sign is negative in a majority of triggered cases, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: In a cooling labor market, PAYEMS declines alongside a higher UNRATE, confirming payroll recession rather than labor supply normalization, which should drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS was 157087.0 and UNRATE was 3.7 on 2023-11-01
failure_condition: If simultaneous payroll contraction and rising unemployment do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the rolling 3-year hit rate exceeds the baseline by less than 0pp, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A stronger GDPC1 release after prior weakness signals real demand acceleration and earnings resilience, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because growth expectations dominate valuation concerns.
failure_condition: If positive GDPC1 surprises do not correspond to positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign agreement falls below 53% over any full business cycle segment, reject the hypothesis.
